content: We can reframe curiosity-driven intrinsic reward from Topic A as a meta-learning objective for memory consolidation in Topic B: an agent dynamically gates which novel exploratory experiences to consolidate into long-term memory based on its intrinsic curiosity signal, rather than just consolidation based on task reward or recency. This creates a self-improving cycle where more curious exploratory patterns that drive future learning are preferentially preserved, while low-curiosity redundant experiences are pruned automatically.
